title: Agent Settings: Timeouts, Idle Messages & Voicemail
lede: Fine-tune how your Smart Agent behaves on a call: maximum call length, silence and idle handling, voicemail (AMD) detection, backchannel, interruptions, and turn detection.

steps.title: Open the Agent Editor
steps.content:
1. In the left navigation, go to Intelligence Center → Smart Agents.
  2. Click an existing agent to open it in the editor (you can switch between Form view and Node view).
  3. The middle column holds the feature panels (Knowledge Base, Voice, Brains, and more), each with its own on/off toggle. Below them you will find Sub Agents / Tasks / Handoffs, Tools / MCP, and Settings.

images.caption: The Smart Agent editor: prompt on the left, feature panels in the middle, Test Agent at the top.
The Smart Agent editor: prompt on the left, feature panels in the middle, Test Agent at the top.

steps.title: Open the Settings Panel
steps.content:
1. Click Settings in the editor.
  2. Use the left rail to move between sections: General, Idle Messages, Voicemail, Speech, Voice Activity Detection, Audio Quality, Background Audio, Interruptions, User Turn Limits, Turn Detection, and Privacy & Recording.

images.caption: The Settings panel with its section rail on the left.
The Settings panel with its section rail on the left.

steps.title: Set Call Length and Silence (General)
steps.content:
• Maximum Duration: the longest a call can run, in seconds. The agent ends the call when this limit is reached.
  • Silence Timeout: how long to wait during silence before the agent ends the call.

steps.title: Configure Idle Messages
steps.content:
1. Open the Idle Messages section and turn it on.
  2. Set the Idle Timeout (seconds of quiet before the agent speaks up) and Max Idle Messages.
  3. Add prompts such as "Are you still there?". Type { to insert a variable like the caller's name.

steps.title: Voicemail Detection (AMD)
steps.content:
• Under Voicemail, turn on Voicemail Detection (AMD) and choose Hang up if reach voicemail or Leave a message if reach voicemail.
  • A voicemail message can include dynamic variables from the conversation.
  • Advanced detection timings (No-Speech Timeout, Detection Timeout, speech/silence thresholds) are available if you need to tune accuracy.

steps.title: Speech, Interruptions & Turn Detection
steps.content:
• Speech: enable Enable Backchannel (Beta) for natural fillers like "uh-huh", and Preemptive Generation so a text-based (STT + LLM + TTS) agent starts thinking while the caller is still speaking.
  • Interruptions: allow or block interruptions and set the minimum words that trigger one.
  • Turn Detection: an audio-based detector decides when the caller has finished talking. These settings are ignored for realtime (speech-to-speech) models, which handle turns themselves.
